title: Arlen Browser Error Audit
review_checklist: Is the primary failure obvious within one screen?
Does the browser response show a concrete next step or fix location when the framework knows one?
Are secondary details visually subordinate to the main error instead of burying it?
Do browser requests get an intentional HTML experience when that would help developer triage?
Are production responses redacted while development responses stay actionable?

scenarios.title: Boomhauer Objective-C Compile Failure
scenarios.description: Watch-mode fallback page after an app-root Objective-C compile error.
review_focus: Can you identify the failing symbol and likely fix without opening the server log first?
Does the recovery guidance feel specific to the watch-mode workflow?
Are warnings and verbose output visually secondary to the primary compiler error?

scenarios.title: Boomhauer EOC Transpile Failure
scenarios.description: Watch-mode fallback page after an app-root template syntax failure.
review_focus: Is the template path and line/column easy to spot?
Does the page make template syntax failures feel first-class rather than generic compile noise?
Is the source snippet prominent enough to resolve the error quickly?

scenarios.title: Development Controller Exception
scenarios.description: Browser request against a development app route that raises an uncaught exception.
review_focus: Is the exception reason immediately visible?
Does the page provide enough context to fix the route without hunting through logs?
Does the page feel intentionally designed, or merely dumped diagnostics in a table?

scenarios.title: Development Browser Validation Failure
scenarios.description: Browser request that fails request-schema validation before controller execution.
review_focus: Would a browser-based developer know which field failed and why?
Should this remain plain text, or graduate to a first-class HTML page?
Does the current fallback match the rest of the development error experience?

scenarios.title: Development Browser Unauthorized
scenarios.description: Browser request against a route with required scopes but no authenticated subject.
review_focus: Is the browser response clear about what sort of auth problem occurred?
Would a developer know whether the issue is missing login, missing token, or missing scope?
Does the plain-text fallback feel acceptable for a browser surface?

scenarios.title: Development Browser Forbidden
scenarios.description: Browser request blocked by a guard action.
review_focus: Does the browser response communicate enough about why the guard failed?
Should browser-facing guard failures expose more actionable developer context in development?
Is the current presentation consistent with other auth and validation failures?

scenarios.title: Development Browser Not Found
scenarios.description: Browser request to an unknown route in development.
review_focus: Would a developer know whether they hit a missing route, wrong verb, or format mismatch?
Should HTML requests get a real 404 page in development?
How much route context is appropriate on this surface?

scenarios.title: Production Browser Internal Error
scenarios.description: Browser request against a production app route that raises an uncaught exception.
review_focus: Is raw JSON the right production browser experience for HTML requests?
Does the response stay properly redacted while remaining debuggable through correlation IDs?
Should production browser requests negotiate a branded HTML error page instead?
